sentence: Subjective:
The patient presents with a chronic history of lower back pain that started about a month ago, which has worsened to the point of being an 8 out of 10 in severity.
The pain is located in the lower back, radiates down the left leg, and affects the hip, side of the leg, side of the calf, and big toe.
The patient has difficulty walking and has been using a cane for support.
The pain is exacerbated by standing, walking, coughing, and sneezing, and is relieved by lying down.
The patient denies any numbness or tingling in the lower extremities, but reports weakness in both legs.
Past medical history:
The patient has a history of a fall from a roof 30 years ago, which may have resulted in a possible fracture.
The patient has been bedridden due to the pain, and has been unable to play football.
Review of systems:
The patient reports no other symptoms in other organs.
Current medications:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam:
The patient has pain to palpation at the L4 vertebrae, and pain with flexion and extension of the back.
The patient has 4 out of 5 strength in the left leg and 5 out of 5 strength in the right leg.
Reflexes are brisk and motor and sensory are intact in both lower extremities.
Test Results:
The MRI shows a disk herniation at the level of L4-L5 vertebrae associated with nerve root impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient presents with acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement causing lower back pain and leg pain.
Problem list: Disk herniation at L4-L5; Nerve root impingement; Lower back pain; Leg pain.
Plan:
I prescribed a course of treatment consisting of an epidural steroid injection, which will be administered today.
I will also refer the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the area.
The patient is to follow up with me in about a month to assess the effectiveness of the treatment.
Follow-up:
The patient will follow up with me in about a month to assess the effectiveness of the treatment.
